metadata.name: Sharktocrab Adapt 1 — counter placement and conditional no-op
metadata.description: Tests two key behaviors of the Adapt ability (CR 701.46a) using Sharktocrab. Phase 1: p1 controls Sharktocrab with no +1/+1 counters — p1 activates Adapt by paying {2}{G}{U} and tapping it. The Conditional effect checks SourceHasNoCountersOfType(PlusOnePlusOne), finds it true, and places 1 +1/+1 counter on Sharktocrab (it becomes a 5/5). Phase 2: p2 controls a Sharktocrab that was pre-placed in the initial state with 1 +1/+1 counter already on it. p1 passes priority to p2, then p2 activates Adapt. The Conditional finds the counter present (condition is false), resolves to Effect::Nothing, and no additional counter is placed. The creature remains a 5/5. This confirms that activation is always legal (per ruling 2019-01-25) but counter placement is gated at resolution time.
metadata.generation_notes: Sharktocrab card definition verified at crates/engine/src/cards/defs/sharktocrab.rs. Abilities: [Keyword(Adapt(1)), Activated { cost: Sequence([Mana({2}{G}{U}), Tap]), effect: Conditional { condition: SourceHasNoCountersOfType(PlusOnePlusOne), if_true: AddCounter { target: Source, count: 1 }, if_false: Nothing } }]. The Keyword entry is not a non-mana activated ability; the Activated entry is ability_index: 0. CR 701.46a: 'Adapt N means put N +1/+1 counters on this permanent unless it already has a +1/+1 counter on it.' Ruling 2019-01-25: activation is always legal; whether counters are placed is determined at resolution. Design: two separate Sharktocrab instances are used (one per player) to avoid object-identity ambiguity. p1 controls a fresh Sharktocrab (no counter, untapped) for Phase 1. p2 controls a pre-adapted Sharktocrab (1 counter pre-loaded via PermanentInitState.counters map, untapped) for Phase 2. Both are simultaneously on the battlefield. After Phase 1 resolves, the active player (p1) regains priority — p1 must pass priority before p2 can activate (CR 116.3). A priority_pass for p1 precedes p2's activation in Phase 2. Mana cost: {2}{G}{U} = colorless: 2, green: 1, blue: 1. Cost::Tap means the creature becomes tapped at activation time (CR 602.2c — costs paid before ability on stack). The 'owner' field is omitted from PermanentInitState (defaults to controller). Counter key format verified in parse_counter_type: 'plus_one_plus_one' maps to CounterType::PlusOnePlusOne. Unit tests: adapt.rs (test_adapt_basic_adds_counters, test_adapt_does_nothing_with_existing_counters) validate these behaviors exhaustively at the Rust level.

script.step_note: Phase 1: p1 activates Adapt on their Sharktocrab (no counters). Conditional passes — 1 +1/+1 counter is placed, making Sharktocrab a 5/5.

actions.description: Initial: p1's Sharktocrab (no counter, untapped); p2's Sharktocrab (1 counter pre-loaded, untapped); stack empty
actions.note: p1's Sharktocrab: 4/4 base, no counters. p2's Sharktocrab: pre-adapted with 1 +1/+1 counter (5/5 effective). Both untapped. p1 holds priority as active player.

CR 602.2: p1 activates p1's Sharktocrab's Adapt ability (ability_index 0 — the sole non-mana activated ability). Cost: {2}{G}{U} + tap. p1 pays {2}{G}{U} from pool and taps p1's Sharktocrab. Sharktocrab remains on the battlefield. The ability is placed on the stack.

actions.description: After p1 activates Adapt: ability on stack, p1's Sharktocrab is tapped
actions.note: p1's Sharktocrab tapped as part of cost payment but remains on the battlefield. Adapt ability is on the stack awaiting resolution.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack resolves.

resolution.note: CR 701.46a: p1's Sharktocrab has no +1/+1 counters — Conditional is true. Effect::AddCounter places 1 +1/+1 counter. CR 121.1: Sharktocrab becomes 5/5 (4/4 base + 1 counter).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Ability resolves. Condition SourceHasNoCountersOfType(PlusOnePlusOne) is satisfied — if_true branch fires, adding 1 counter. Ability ceases to exist after resolution (CR 608.2n).

actions.description: After Phase 1 resolution: stack empty; both Sharktocrab on battlefield; life totals unchanged
actions.note: p1's Sharktocrab now has 1 +1/+1 counter (5/5). p2's Sharktocrab unchanged (still 1 counter from initial state). No SBA needed.

script.step_note: Phase 2: p1 passes priority to p2. p2 activates Adapt on their already-adapted Sharktocrab (1 counter). Conditional fails — Effect::Nothing fires, no counter placed.

actions.description: Before Phase 2: stack empty; p2's Sharktocrab untapped with 1 counter; p1 holds priority
actions.note: p2's Sharktocrab is untapped (it was never tapped — only p1's was tapped in Phase 1). It has 1 +1/+1 counter. p1 holds priority and must pass before p2 can act.
CR 116.3a: p1 passes priority. p2 now holds priority and may activate abilities.

CR 602.2: p2 activates p2's Sharktocrab's Adapt ability. Per ruling 2019-01-25, activation is always legal regardless of counter state. p2 pays {2}{G}{U} and taps p2's Sharktocrab. The ability is placed on the stack.

actions.description: After p2 activates Adapt: ability on stack; p2's Sharktocrab is now tapped
actions.note: Ability is on the stack. p2's Sharktocrab already has 1 +1/+1 counter — Conditional will evaluate to false at resolution.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack resolves.

resolution.note: CR 701.46a: p2's Sharktocrab already has a +1/+1 counter — Conditional is false. Effect::Nothing fires. No counter is placed. The {2}{G}{U} mana was still spent and p2's Sharktocrab is still tapped (costs are always paid regardless of whether the effect fires — ruling 2019-01-25).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Ability resolves. Condition SourceHasNoCountersOfType(PlusOnePlusOne) is NOT satisfied — if_false branch (Effect::Nothing) fires. No counters added. Ability ceases to exist (CR 608.2n).

actions.description: Final: stack empty; both Sharktocrab still on battlefield; life totals unchanged; no extra counter was placed on p2's Sharktocrab
actions.note: CR 701.46a: Adapt only places counters if the creature has none. After Phase 2 resolved with Effect::Nothing, p2's Sharktocrab still has exactly 1 +1/+1 counter (effective 5/5) — the same count it had before activation. The mana was spent and the creature tapped, but no counter was added. Phase 1 (p1's Sharktocrab) demonstrated the counter-placement path. Phase 2 (p2's Sharktocrab) demonstrated the conditional no-op path.
